food_subgroup: Prawn and pork rib broth noodle soup

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has no specific concept for Singapore Prawn Noodle Soup. Nearest parent is a mixed noodle soup concept. A new FoodOn concept is recommended.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite noodle soup dishes — prawn and pork broth noodle soup (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical bowl of Singapore Prawn Noodle Soup weighs approximately 560g and consists of yellow egg noodles and rice vermicelli in a rich sweet prawn and pork rib stock, topped with fresh prawns, pork ribs, thin pork belly slices, half-boiled egg, bean sprouts, and kangkung, garnished with crispy fried shallots and served with sambal belacan on the side.

nutrient_source.title: Prawn Noodle Soup — HPB Singapore Foodo Database; no exact code found; values adapted from HPB prawn noodle and hae mee composition data
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Prawn Noodle Soup entry found in HPB Foodo under a distinct food code. Values adapted from HPB prawn-based noodle soup component data and prawn stock analysis.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — mixed noodles (egg noodles + rice vermicelli); composite GI estimated.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 3220mg per bowl (560g) — significantly exceeding the 2300mg daily recommended limit, driven by the long-simmered prawn and pork broth.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid finishing the broth and request reduced sambal belacan; consuming only the noodles and toppings while leaving most of the broth will substantially reduce sodium intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Mixed noodle base has a moderate GI (~51); the large bowl serving size means a moderate-high carbohydrate load of approximately 81g.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should request a half-portion of noodles and additional kangkung, and consider the pork ribs and prawns as the primary nutritional focus rather than the noodle base.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Prawns provide omega-3 fatty acids; kangkung adds anti-inflammatory antioxidants; pork rib protein supports muscle maintenance.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Prawn Noodle Soup is a suitable meal for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; the prawn and pork protein content is beneficial, and patients should focus on the solid ingredients while limiting the high-sodium broth.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Singapore Prawn Noodle Soup (Hae Mee) is a hawker classic of yellow egg noodles and rice vermicelli served in a deeply flavourful prawn and pork rib stock that has been simmered for hours with prawn shells, lemongrass, and spices to develop an intensely sweet, umami-rich broth. The bowl is topped with fresh whole prawns, fork-tender pork ribs, thin pork belly slices, half a hard-boiled egg, blanched bean sprouts and kangkung, and crispy fried shallots, with a side of house-made sambal belacan. It is one of Singapore's most enduring hawker dishes, associated with early morning queues and long-established family stalls.
common_user_queries: how many calories in prawn noodle soup
can a diabetic eat prawn noodles
how much sodium in prawn noodle soup
hae mee dry vs soup which is healthier

confidence_reason: No exact Prawn Noodle Soup entry found in HPB Foodo. Values adapted from HPB component analysis. Confidence medium.
notes: Not halal due to pork ribs and pork belly. Halal prawn mee with chicken substitutes exists. Sodium from broth is very high — the broth alone accounts for the majority of sodium. A dry (gon lo) version with less broth would be lower in sodium.
